monitors.command: Monitor: bash <scratchpad>/ws31-inbox-watch.sh — 45s poll, emits only lines with id>baseline (persistent:true)
monitors.note: RE-ARMED 2026-07-04 on operator instruction (baseline se-inbox-47). Prior task bxw6qhsg7 dropped during the ~2h account-wide throttle window. LESSON: bg watchers die on session teardown/throttle with no signal → ALSO manually `tail` the inbox at the top of every turn; the watcher is a backup, not the primary guarantee. On re-arm the baseline = current-max, so it will NOT replay messages that arrived while dead — always manually drain the gap (this re-arm missed se-44..47 until manual read).

monitors.purpose: catch review verdicts (Reviewing→Closed / Reviewing→Executing rework) for WI-1154/1155 — LOOP GUARD: a Reviewing→Executing re-bounce means ESCALATE to orchestrator (do not re-loop >1)
monitors.command: Monitor: bash <scratchpad>/ws31-stage-watch.sh — 90s poll of data source 36fd1119-9955-4684-8bfe-deb145e6a21f filtered by Workstream relation, emits Stage diffs (persistent:true)
monitors.note: DOWN BY DESIGN 2026-07-04: flapped exit-255 THREE times (bfkehik9d/bg03ioc8b/b1ab0qkm6) — the Notion-curl poller is unstable under the current teardown pattern, unlike the stable file-poll inbox watcher. Per my own fallback rule I stopped chasing it. STAGE CHANNEL = MANUAL: query the Cosmo Stage directly when needed. Zero-risk right now — nothing is in Reviewing awaiting a verdict (wave undispatched). RE-ARM this watcher the moment the wave is dispatched and WIs can reach Reviewing (that's when stage-diff detection matters). Stateless; Stage is a SELECT → jq .properties.Stage.select.name.
